title: qemu: uncaught target signal 11 (Segmentation fault) - core dumped - When running AMD64 Python PyArrow code on Mac
body:
### Issue Description

Not sure Is this issue with Podman, Python or Pyarrow.

Trying to run container image that was built for AMD64 on a Mac. Importing `pyarrow` crashes the process. https://github.com/apache/arrow/issues/46276

After that I need to manually kill the podman process using `kill -9`. Normal kill does not work.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman run --arch amd64 python:3.11-slim bash -c 'pip install pyarrow==20.0.0; python -c "import pyarrow"'`


### Describe the results you received

```
% podman run --arch amd64 python:3.11-slim bash -c 'pip install pyarrow==20.0.0; python -c "import pyarrow"'
Collecting pyarrow==20.0.0
  Downloading pyarrow-20.0.0-cp311-cp311-manylinux_2_28_x86_64.whl.metadata (3.3 kB)
Downloading pyarrow-20.0.0-cp311-cp311-manylinux_2_28_x86_64.whl (42.3 MB)
   ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 42.3/42.3 MB 29.1 MB/s eta 0:00:00
Installing collected packages: pyarrow
Successfully installed pyarrow-20.0.0
WARNING: Running pip as the 'root' user can result in broken permissions and conflicting behaviour with the system package manager. It is recommended to use a virtual environment instead: https://pip.pypa.io/warnings/venv

[notice] A new release of pip is available: 24.0 -> 25.1
[notice] To update, run: pip install --upgrade pip
qemu: uncaught target signal 11 (Segmentation fault) - core dumped
```

### Describe the results you expected

I expect to see no segfaults.
I expect podman process to not become stuck where it cannot be killed using `kill`.

comments.body:
Hi @Ark-kun 

I can't reproduce this on my M1 Macbook running Podman 5.4.2 or when building from main branch. 

On a mac the container operation is actually ran inside a Fedora CoreOS VM and Rosetta is used for binary translation. 

When the VM is created we attach the Apple Rosetta device, inside the VM we mount the device and configure binfmt_misc to use Apples Rosetta translation layer for any x86_64 binaries.

Using your example I can test this:
```
podman run --rm -d --arch amd64 python:3.11-slim bash -c 'pip install pyarrow==20.0.0; python -c "import pyarrow" && sleep 1000'
podman machine ssh
[core@localhost ~]$ ps -elf |grep rosetta
4 S core        2773    2771  0  80   0 - 44712 do_wai 13:10 ?        00:00:00 /mnt/rosetta /usr/bin/bash -c pip install pyarrow==20.0.0; python -c "import pyarrow" && sleep 1000
```

When running a container with the --arch flag Podman is configured to pull the container image with the matching arch, binaries inside that pulled image will be compiled for that arch. When the binary is ran, if the binary files magic number matches the x86_64 bitmask we configure in `/proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc/rosetta` then the binary will be ran through Apples Rosetta binary translation

You can verify if Rosetta is enable(default) by checking:
`podman machine inspect --format {{.Rosetta}}`

Please let me know what you think.

I'm on M4 Macbook. Pretty new, latest updates. Although, company-issued.
```
% podman machine inspect --format {{.Rosetta}}
false
```
I'm not sure why Rosetta is turned off here.

I think I've installed Podman myself (judging by dates) via Homebrew.
I'm very new to Podman and I have not done any tinkering or config changes. Just `podman machine start` and things like `podman build/run/push/tag`.

Hi @Ark-kun, installing Podman from Homebrew is an unsupported method. Can you try removing the current installation and install via the pkg installer or build from source? You can find the pkg installer in our Releases page.

I have Rosetta acceleration operational with Homebrew version (same with selfbuilt versions) on M4 Pro. I had to install Rosetta specifically, because it was not installed out of the box on a fresh OS install.

@Ark-kun do you have Rosetta installed on your laptop? You might need to delete and recreate podman machine after installing Rosetta.

please install the latest release from upstream.  could you also provide `which podman | xargs file` and `podman machine ls` ?  if you are running the applehv provider, then everything should be in order.  also cannot reproduce.

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).

>do you have Rosetta installed on your laptop?

I think Rosetta exists on my laptop:

```
ark@Mac tmp.N0mWfbNKkF % ls -la /Library/Apple/usr/libexec/oah/libRosettaRuntime
-rwxr-xr-x  1 root  wheel  464320 Mar  7 19:43 /Library/Apple/usr/libexec/oah/libRosettaRuntime
ark@Mac tmp.N0mWfbNKkF % cat /Library/Apple/usr/share/rosetta/rosetta
translation is done 
as preferred long in advance
rosetta idles
```

@baude 

```
% which podman | xargs file
/opt/homebrew/bin/podman: Mach-O 64-bit executable arm64
```

```
% podman machine ls
NAME                    VM TYPE     CREATED       LAST UP            CPUS        MEMORY      DISK SIZE
podman-machine-default  applehv     2 months ago  Currently running  6           8GiB        60GiB
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I'm also running into this issue. Rosetta is enabled:
```
% podman machine inspect --format {{.Rosetta}}
true

```

yet:
```
 > [builder 5/9] RUN go mod download:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
0.157 SIGSEGV: segmentation violation                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
0.157 PC=0x43a8ae m=4 sigcode=1 addr=0xffffffff404cbdf0                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
0.157                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
0.157 goroutine 0 gp=0xc00008a1c0 m=4 mp=0xc000088008 [idle]:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             
0.158 runtime.netpoll(0xc00003c000?)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      
0.158   runtime/netpoll_epoll.go:169 +0x24e fp=0xc000079db0 sp=0xc000079728 pc=0x43a8ae                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
0.158 runtime.findRunnable()                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
0.158   runtime/proc.go:3623 +0x8c5 fp=0xc000079f28 sp=0xc000079db0 pc=0x447605
0.158 runtime.schedule()
0.158   runtime/proc.go:4038 +0xb1 fp=0xc000079f60 sp=0xc000079f28 pc=0x448bd1
0.158 runtime.park_m(0xc00008a8c0)
0.158   runtime/proc.go:4162 +0x285 fp=0xc000079fc0 sp=0xc000079f60 pc=0x449085
0.159 runtime.mcall()
0.159   runtime/asm_amd64.s:459 +0x4e fp=0xc000079fd8 sp=0xc000079fc0 pc=0x47b7ae
```

what additional information can I provide?

EDIT: my crash is caused by using `docker buildx build` with podman, which uses qemu rather than rosetta. I'll look for or file a separate issue.

I'm seeing a similar issue as well. I'm seeing the `qemu: uncaught target signal 11 (Segmentation fault) - core dumped` error when building a container (deploying dacpacs to an mssql server database - a container image used with integration tests). I'm not sure if its the sqlservr process or .net's sqlpackage process that's failing, but rosetta *is* enabled and it's still using qemu.

The error only occurs when using a new podman machine created with podman version 5.6.0. I have an old machine created under podman 5.1.2, and under that machine no error is encountered. The new machine *does not* have the `/mnt/rosetta` and `/mnt/rosettad` directories, but the old one does.

If I run the OP's example command `podman run --arch amd64 python:3.11-slim bash -c 'pip install pyarrow==20.0.0; python -c "import pyarrow"'` under my old machine, it works fine.  Under the new machine I get the segmentation fault.
Running into same issue with podman machine 5.6

Same issue with M4 and Podman v5.6.2; it was working some weeks ago.
I tried both applehv and LibKrun, none worked.
@eavilaes I've downgraded the podman machine version, only option unfortunately

I am having the same problem on an Apple M4 (Sequoia 15.7.2) with Podman 5.7.0.
I tried both `libkrun` and `applehv`: `libkrun` is not able to enable Rosetta at all, but `applehv` can:

```bash
❯ podman machine inspect --format {{.Rosetta}}
true
```

```bash
❯ podman machine info
host:
    arch: arm64
    currentmachine: podman-machine-default
    defaultmachine: ""
    eventsdir: /var/folders/y1/39mrbp2175d56pdhhp99wvw80000gn/T/storage-run-501/podman
    machineconfigdir: /Users/maffioll/.config/containers/podman/machine/applehv
    machineimagedir: /Users/maffioll/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/applehv
    machinestate: Running
    numberofmachines: 1
    os: darwin
    vmtype: applehv
version:
    apiversion: 5.7.0
    version: 5.7.0
    goversion: go1.25.4
    gitcommit: 0370128fc8dcae93533334324ef838db8f8da8cb
    builttime: Tue Nov 11 19:53:03 2025
    built: 1762887183
    buildorigin: pkginstaller
    osarch: darwin/arm64
    os: darwin
```

But then I build my image and:

```
Installing collected packages: mpmath, typing-extensions, sympy, pillow, numpy, networkx, MarkupSafe, fsspec, filelock, jinja2, torch, torchvision, torchaudio
Successfully installed MarkupSafe-2.1.5 filelock-3.19.1 fsspec-2025.9.0 jinja2-3.1.6 mpmath-1.3.0 networkx-3.5 numpy-2.3.3 pillow-11.3.0 sympy-1.14.0 torch-2.9.1+cpu torchaudio-2.9.1+cpu torchvision-0.24.1+cpu typing-extensions-4.15.0
WARNING: Running pip as the 'root' user can result in broken permissions and conflicting behaviour with the system package manager. It is recommended to use a virtual environment instead: https://pip.pypa.io/warnings/venv
qemu: uncaught target signal 11 (Segmentation fault) - core dumped
Segmentation fault (core dumped)
```

Installed podman and podman desktop NOT via brew - still same error.

`podman machine inspect --format {{.Rosetta}}` will give you false until you re-create Podman machine with Provider Type option set to "Apple Hypervisor" instead of default "default GPU enabled (LibKrun)". But even having `podman machine inspect --format {{.Rosetta}}` returning true doesn't help at all

I also have similar issues - running an x86_64 container on my MacBook M1 Pro.
Had been working fine with podman 5.2.5 and then 5.4.2 for several months, upgraded to 5.7.1 and thereafter got the qemu seg fault errors.
Tried various flags/config etc to ensure rosetta was installed and force applehv+rosetta usage but nothing helped.
Downgrading to 5.2.5 is what worked for me. I did not try 5.4.2 for internal reasons...
I should mention that the x86_64 server application in the container was a binary built with C/C++.

Also running into the same issue while trying to compile a Rust binary on amd64 architecture. I'm running podman 5.7.1 as well, and I haven't tried downgrading the version. Ensured Rosetta is running, installed and configured for the podman machine.

> Also running into the same issue while trying to compile a Rust binary on amd64 architecture. I'm running podman 5.7.1 as well, and I haven't tried downgrading the version. Ensured Rosetta is running, installed and configured for the podman machine.

I spent a couple of days trying to get my container to work with 5.7.1 - tried various config settings / env vars / conf file etc etc. In the end, the only way I could get it to work was downgrade.

> I also have similar issues - running an x86_64 container on my MacBook M1 Pro. Had been working fine with podman 5.2.5 and then 5.4.2 for several months, upgraded to 5.7.1 and thereafter got the qemu seg fault errors. Tried various flags/config etc to ensure rosetta was installed and force applehv+rosetta usage but nothing helped. Downgrading to 5.2.5 is what worked for me. I did not try 5.4.2 for internal reasons... I should mention that the x86_64 server application in the container was a binary built with C/C++.

I can also confirm that 5.2.5 resolves issues like `Fatal Python error: Segmentation fault` that currently exist with 5.7.1

QEMU apparently has various segfaults...

Even if rosetta is enabled at the config level, it now has to be manually enabled inside machine-os with
```sh
> podman machine ssh "sudo touch /etc/containers/enable-rosetta"
```

See:
1] https://blog.podman.io/2025/08/podman-5-6-released-rosetta-status-update/
2] https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-os/blob/eb4357565d9a3c7408260709c81e5d835cf40522/podman-image/rosetta-activation.service#L9
3] https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-os/issues/212

i confirm 
`> podman machine ssh "sudo touch /etc/containers/enable-rosetta"`
and restarting the podman machine working and then running the amd64 container on MacOS M4 worked.

> i confirm `> podman machine ssh "sudo touch /etc/containers/enable-rosetta"` and restarting the podman machine working and then running the amd64 container on MacOS M4 worked.

This didn't help with podman 5.7.1 on M1 using LibKrun, while with Apple HyperVisor it produces this error now while rebuilding a repository within a VS Code Dev Container:

```bash
WARNING: image platform (linux/amd64) does not match the expected platform (linux/arm64)
rosetta error: unhandled auxillary vector type 29
```

> i confirm `> podman machine ssh "sudo touch /etc/containers/enable-rosetta"` and restarting the podman machine working and then running the amd64 container on MacOS M4 worked.

I had also tried this previously with 5.7.1 on M1 and it had not helped... I can't remember the error

> > i confirm `> podman machine ssh "sudo touch /etc/containers/enable-rosetta"` and restarting the podman machine working and then running the amd64 container on MacOS M4 worked.
> 
> This didn't help with podman 5.7.1 on M1 using LibKrun, while with Apple HyperVisor it produces this error now while rebuilding a repository within a VS Code Dev Container:
> 
> WARNING: image platform (linux/amd64) does not match the expected platform (linux/arm64)
> rosetta error: unhandled auxillary vector type 29

After upgrading my M1 machine from macOS Sequoia to macOS Tahoe (26.4.1) I can confirm that qemu error is gone, and the terminal/podman works even faster! I just had to make sure to create the Podman Machine using `Apple HyperVisor` as the "Provider Type", as the issue persists with `default GPU enabled (LibKrun)`. There was no need to run any `podman` commands noted above in this thread.

Ran into the same segfault issue on M4 mac with macOS Tahoe 26.5.1 while trying to build cross-platform images. Switching the podman machine from LibKrun to Apple HyperVisor like mentioned above resolved the issue.
